pattern.imageEvidence: espresso cart service in a bright event room with guests, barista, cart equipment, and menu setup visible
pattern.styleContract: Warm event hospitality with package-fit proof, a bright event-room espresso cart scene, citrus action accents, and a readable menu ticket as the signature move.

tagline: A tidy espresso bar that makes the room smell like yes.

hero.title: Cartwright Coffee Co. mobile espresso cart hero image
hero.alt: A barista preparing espresso drinks at a wood coffee cart during a bright indoor event with guests in the background.

contact.serviceArea: Espresso cart service for weddings, offices, open houses, showers, markets, and private events across the central metro and nearby suburbs

copy.eyebrow: Mobile espresso bar for event hosts
copy.heroText: Cartwright rolls in with a polished espresso cart, friendly baristas, and a tight drink menu that keeps guests moving, caffeinated, and gently impressed.
copy.introTitle: A small bar that carries the whole room.
copy.introText: We bring the machine, beans, dairy and non-dairy options, cups, cleanup rhythm, and a menu that feels special without turning your event into a checkout counter.
copy.proofTitle: Built for hosts who notice details
copy.quoteTitle: Want the cart at your event?
copy.quoteText: Check availability with date, venue, guest count, service window, and whether you want espresso only or a fuller pastry-and-cold-drink spread. We will reply with the best-fit package.

services.title: Espresso Social
services.text: A compact espresso and latte menu for showers, open houses, team mornings, and events where the cart should feel easy and elegant.

services.title: Full Barista Bar
services.text: Espresso drinks, drip coffee, tea, milk alternatives, flavored syrups, and a service pace planned around your guest count.

services.title: Coffee plus Bites
services.text: A hosted coffee cart with pastries, sparkling water, iced options, and a tidy display that photographs beautifully without hogging the floor plan.

process.title: Send the event basics
process.text: Share the date, location, guest count, service window, power access, and any dietary notes we should plan around.

process.title: Match the menu
process.text: We recommend a cart format, staffing level, and drink list so the line keeps moving and the host is not troubleshooting milk.

process.title: Roll in and pour
process.text: The cart arrives polished, self-contained, and calm, then leaves the area clean after the final cup is handed off.

proof.label: minimum service window for small private events
quiet cart styling that blends into your event

notes: Works: a readable menu ticket. gives the page a memorable shape.
Proof: espresso cart service in a bright event room with guests, barista, cart equipment, and menu setup visible. anchors the niche in the first viewport.
Action: Check your date is the main conversion path.

brandBrief.accentBehavior: sun stays reserved for Check your date, active proof cues, and key booking moments.
brandBrief.imageProof: espresso cart service in a bright event room with guests, barista, cart equipment, and menu setup visible.
brandBrief.avoidLookingLike: Avoid a generic package-menu-board recolor; do not repeat stock hero framing, copied card rhythm, or vague local-service claims.

serviceDetails.turnaround: 90 min: minimum service window for small private events.
serviceDetails.whatToSend: Share the event date, venue or address, guest count, setup window, power/water rules, and the package style you are considering.
serviceDetails.prepNote: Share the date, location, guest count, service window, power access, and any dietary notes we should plan around.
serviceDetails.serviceRhythm: Date-window flow for Espresso cart service for weddings, offices, open houses, showers, markets, and private events across the central metro and nearby suburbs: date fit, setup rules, guest flow, and hold status come before package pressure.
serviceDetails.objectionAnswer: For coffee cart catering service, guest count, menu pace, service footprint, prep access, and cleanup timing are matched before the package is recommended.
